ssc-photo-signature-resizer.whatItIs: This tool helps you resize and compress both your photo and signature to meet the exact requirements for SSC exams. Staff Selection Commission forms typically require a passport photo between 20-50KB and a signature between 10-20KB, and this utility ensures your files fit these specifications perfectly.

Compress photos to the 20-50KB range automatically

Process multiple images in one session

howToUse.name: How to Use SSC Photo and Signature Resizer

steps.name: Upload Your Photo
steps.text: Click the upload button or drag and drop your passport photo into the designated area. The tool accepts JPG or PNG formats.

steps.name: Set Photo Dimensions
steps.text: The tool automatically sets the dimensions to 350x450 pixels for SSC. You can adjust if your specific exam requires different measurements.

steps.name: Compress to Required Size
steps.text: Use the compression slider to reduce your photo to between 20-50KB while maintaining clarity. The tool shows the file size in real-time.

steps.name: Upload and Process Signature
steps.text: Repeat the process for your signature, targeting the 10-20KB range. Crop any excess white space around your signature first for best results.

steps.name: Download and Verify
steps.text: Download both processed files. Double-check the file sizes and dimensions match SSC requirements before submitting your application.

faqs.question: What are the exact photo requirements for SSC CGL?
faqs.answer: SSC CGL requires a recent passport-size color photograph in JPG format, typically 350x450 pixels, with file size between 20KB and 50KB. The background should be white or light-colored.

faqs.question: Can I use a scanned signature for SSC forms?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use a scanned signature. Make sure it's clear, in black ink on white paper, and scanned at high resolution before compressing to 10-20KB.

faqs.question: Why does my photo look blurry after compression?
faqs.answer: If your photo appears blurry, the original image may have been too small or low resolution. Start with a high-quality photo (at least 600x800 pixels) for best results after compression.

faqs.question: Does this tool work for SSC CHSL and MTS exams too?
faqs.answer: Yes, the photo and signature requirements for SSC CHSL, MTS, and most other SSC exams are similar. This tool is configured to handle all common SSC exam specifications.

faqs.question: What if my compressed photo is still over 50KB?
faqs.answer: If your photo is still too large, try cropping it more tightly around your face and shoulders, or reduce the image quality slightly. The tool provides real-time feedback on file size.

faqs.question: Is there a limit to how many photos I can process?
faqs.answer: No, you can process as many photos and signatures as you need. The tool works entirely in your browser with no usage limits.

howToUse.name: How to Use UPSC Photo Resizer 350x350

steps.name: Select Your Photo
steps.text: Upload a recent passport-size photograph from your device. The photo should have a plain light background and show your full face clearly.

steps.name: Automatic Resizing
steps.text: The tool automatically resizes your photo to 350x350 pixels. If your photo isn't square, it will crop to maintain the 1:1 aspect ratio.

steps.name: Adjust Crop if Needed
steps.text: If the automatic crop doesn't center your face properly, use the drag handles to reposition the crop area before finalizing.

steps.name: Compress to Required Size
steps.text: Adjust the compression level to meet UPSC's file size requirements (typically under 300KB). The tool displays the current file size.

steps.name: Download and Save
steps.text: Download your resized photo. Save it with a clear filename like 'UPSC_Photo_YourName.jpg' for easy identification during form submission.

faqs.question: What is the exact photo size required for UPSC IAS exam?
faqs.answer: UPSC requires a passport-size photograph of 350x350 pixels in JPG format. The file size should typically be between 20KB and 300KB, with a plain white or light-colored background.

faqs.question: Can I wear glasses in my UPSC application photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear glasses, but make sure there's no reflection on the lenses and your eyes are clearly visible. Avoid tinted or sunglasses.

faqs.question: Does UPSC accept photos with borders or frames?
faqs.answer: No, UPSC does not accept photos with borders, frames, or any other decorations. The photo should be a plain passport-style image.

faqs.question: What should I do if my photo is rejected by UPSC portal?
faqs.answer: If your photo is rejected, check the error message for specific issues. Common problems include wrong dimensions, file size too large, or poor image quality. Use this tool to correct these issues.

faqs.question: Can I use a selfie for UPSC application?
faqs.answer: While you can use a selfie, it's not recommended. A professional passport photo with proper lighting and a plain background is more likely to be accepted without issues.

faqs.question: How recent should my UPSC application photo be?
faqs.answer: Your photo should be recent, typically not older than 3-6 months. It should accurately represent your current appearance.

howToUse.name: How to Use NEET Photo & Signature Resizer

steps.name: Upload Your Passport Photo
steps.text: Select a recent passport-size photograph with a white background. The photo should show your head and shoulders clearly.

steps.name: Set NEET Photo Specifications
steps.text: The tool automatically configures the dimensions to NEET standards (3.5cm x 4.5cm). Adjust the crop area to center your face properly.

steps.name: Compress Photo to Required Size
steps.text: Use the compression controls to reduce your photo to between 10-200KB. NEET typically requires photos under 200KB but check your specific notification.

steps.name: Upload and Process Signature
steps.text: Upload your signature image. The tool will resize and compress it to the 4-30KB range required by NEET. Sign on white paper with black ink for best results.

steps.name: Download Both Files

faqs.question: What are the exact photo dimensions for NEET 2026?
faqs.answer: For NEET 2026, the photograph should be in JPG format with dimensions 3.5cm (width) x 4.5cm (height). The file size should be between 10KB and 200KB.

faqs.question: Can I use a colored background for NEET photo?
faqs.answer: No, NEET requires a white background for the passport photograph. Any colored or patterned background will result in rejection of your application.

faqs.question: What type of signature does NEET accept?
faqs.answer: NEET accepts a clear scanned image of your signature in running handwriting. It should be on white paper with black or dark blue ink, and the file size should be between 4KB and 30KB.

faqs.question: Can I wear a cap or religious headwear in my NEET photo?
faqs.answer: Religious headwear is allowed only if it doesn't cover your face. Caps, hats, or any other headwear that obscures your face or hairline are not permitted.

faqs.question: Why was my NEET photo rejected even after using this tool?
faqs.answer: Common reasons for rejection include poor lighting, shadows on the face, red eyes, or not following the dress code (avoid uniforms or similar clothing). Ensure your photo has even lighting and a neutral expression.

faqs.question: Can I edit my photo after uploading to NEET portal?
faqs.answer: Once you've submitted your application, you cannot edit the photo. However, during the application correction window (if available), you may be able to replace it. Always verify your photo before final submission.

faqs.answer: The UPSC Photo Resizer 350x350 is specifically for resizing to that exact dimension, while this UPSC Photo Resizer also handles signature resizing and compression for both photos and signatures in one tool.

faqs.question: Can I use the same photo for multiple UPSC exams?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use the same photo for multiple UPSC exams as long as it meets the specifications for each exam and your appearance hasn't changed significantly.

faqs.question: What should I wear for my UPSC application photo?
faqs.answer: Wear light-colored, plain clothing. Avoid uniforms, dark colors, or patterns. The photo should clearly show your face and shoulders against the plain background.

faqs.question: Can I smile in my UPSC application photo?
faqs.answer: You should maintain a neutral expression with your mouth closed. A slight smile is generally acceptable, but avoid broad smiles or laughter that might distort your facial features.

faqs.question: How do I ensure my signature is accepted by UPSC?
faqs.answer: Your signature should be clear and legible, in your normal handwriting. Use black ink on white paper, scan at high resolution, and ensure it's not too small or too large. The file size should be between 10-40KB.

faqs.question: What if my photo doesn't meet UPSC requirements after using this tool?
faqs.answer: If your photo is rejected, check the specific error message. Common issues include wrong dimensions, file size too large, poor quality, or not following the background requirements. Re-process your photo with adjusted settings.

faqs.question: Can I use a black and white photo for SSC exams?
faqs.answer: SSC typically requires color photographs. Black and white photos are generally not accepted unless specifically mentioned in the exam notification.

faqs.question: What should be the background color for SSC photo?
faqs.answer: The background should be plain white or a very light color. Avoid patterned, dark, or colored backgrounds as they may lead to rejection of your application.

faqs.question: Can I wear spectacles in my SSC application photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear spectacles, but ensure there's no glare on the lenses and your eyes are clearly visible. Avoid tinted glasses or sunglasses.

faqs.question: How can I reduce my photo size without losing quality?
faqs.answer: Start with a high-resolution photo (at least 600x800 pixels). The tool uses smart compression to reduce file size while maintaining visual clarity. Avoid excessive compression that makes the photo blurry.

faqs.question: What if my photo is still over 50KB after compression?
faqs.answer: If your photo is still too large, try cropping it more tightly around your face and shoulders, or slightly reduce the image quality. You can also try starting with a smaller original image.

howToUse.name: How to Resize Signature Under 20KB

steps.name: Scan or Photograph Your Signature
steps.text: Create a digital image of your signature. For best results, sign on plain white paper with black ink, then scan at 300 DPI or take a clear photo in good lighting.

steps.name: Upload Signature Image
steps.text: Upload your signature image to the tool. The signature should be clear and dark against the white background.

steps.name: Crop Excess Space
steps.text: Use the crop tool to remove any unnecessary white space around your signature. This helps reduce the file size significantly.

steps.name: Compress to Under 20KB
steps.text: Adjust the compression level until your signature file is under 20KB. The tool shows the current file size in real-time.

steps.name: Download and Test
steps.text: Download your compressed signature. Open the file to ensure it's still clear and legible before using it for official submissions.

faqs.question: What is the best way to create a digital signature?
faqs.answer: Sign on plain white paper with a black gel pen or marker for a dark, clear signature. Scan it at 300 DPI or take a photo in natural light with no shadows. Avoid using ballpoint pens as they may create faint signatures.

faqs.question: Can I use a typed name instead of a signature?
faqs.answer: No, most official forms require your actual handwritten signature, not a typed name. A typed name may be considered invalid and could lead to rejection of your application.

faqs.question: Why does my signature look pixelated after compression?
faqs.answer: Pixelation usually occurs when the original signature image is too small or low resolution. Start with a high-quality scan or photo (at least 600x200 pixels) for best results after compression.

faqs.question: What if my signature is still over 20KB?
faqs.answer: If your signature is still too large, try cropping more tightly around the signature itself, reducing the image dimensions, or using a slightly lower compression quality. You can also try re-scanning at a lower DPI.

faqs.question: Can I use this tool for multiple different applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use this tool for any application that requires a signature under 20KB. The processed signature file can be reused for multiple forms.

faqs.question: Is a digital signature legally valid?
faqs.answer: A scanned image of your handwritten signature is generally accepted as valid for most online applications. However, for legal documents, you may need a digital signature certificate. Always check the specific requirements of the form you're submitting.

faqs.question: Can I wear a white shirt in my NEET photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear a white shirt, but make sure it contrasts with the white background so your face and clothing are clearly visible. Avoid shirts that blend into the background.

faqs.question: What should be the expression in my NEET photo?
faqs.answer: Maintain a neutral expression with your mouth closed. Avoid smiling, frowning, or any exaggerated expressions. Your eyes should be open and clearly visible.

faqs.question: Can I use a photo with a shadow on my face for NEET?
faqs.answer: No, photos with shadows on the face are typically rejected. Ensure your photo is taken in even, diffused lighting with no shadows on your face or background.

faqs.question: How can I ensure my NEET photo is not rejected?
faqs.answer: Follow these guidelines: recent photo (not older than 6 months), plain white background, neutral expression, no shadows, proper lighting, and clear facial features. Use this tool to ensure correct dimensions and file size.

faqs.question: Can I use the same photo for NEET and JEE?
faqs.answer: You can use the same photo if it meets the requirements for both exams. However, NEET and JEE may have slightly different specifications, so always verify the photo meets each exam's specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the photo requirements for IBPS PO exam?
faqs.answer: For IBPS PO, the photograph should be in JPG format with dimensions typically 200x230 pixels. The file size should be between 20KB and 50KB, with a plain white background.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IBPS and SBI photo requirements?
faqs.answer: IBPS and SBI photo requirements are generally similar, but there may be slight differences in dimensions or file size limits. Always check the specific notification for your exam. This tool can handle both requirements.

faqs.question: Can I wear glasses in my bank exam photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear glasses, but ensure there's no glare on the lenses and your eyes are clearly visible. Avoid tinted glasses or sunglasses.

faqs.question: What should be the background in my IBPS photo?
faqs.answer: IBPS requires a plain white background for the passport photograph. Avoid patterned, colored, or dark backgrounds as they may lead to rejection of your application.

faqs.question: What if my bank exam photo is rejected?
faqs.answer: If your photo is rejected, check the error message for specific issues. Common problems include wrong dimensions, file size too large, poor image quality, or not following the background requirements. Use this tool to correct these issues.

faqs.question: Can I use a selfie for IBPS application?
faqs.answer: While you can use a selfie, it's not recommended. A professional passport photo with proper lighting, plain background, and neutral expression is more likely to be accepted without issues.

ibps-photo-resizer.seoDescription: Bank Exam Photo Resizer - Free online tool to resize and compress photos and signatures for IBPS PO, Clerk, SBI, and other banking exams. Meet exact requirements with browser-based processing.

pan-card-photo-resizer.whatItIs: This tool helps you resize and compress your photograph for PAN card applications, both for new applications and updates. It ensures your photo meets the exact specifications required by the Income Tax Department for PAN card processing.
features: Resize photos to PAN card requirements
Maintains image quality and facial clarity

howToUse.name: How to Use PAN Card Photo Resizer

steps.name: Upload Your Photograph
steps.text: Select a recent passport-size color photograph. For PAN card, the photo should have a plain white background and show your full face clearly from the front.

steps.name: Set PAN Card Dimensions
steps.text: The tool automatically configures the output to PAN card dimensions (typically 3.5cm x 2.5cm or 350x250 pixels). The aspect ratio will be maintained during resizing.

steps.name: Adjust Crop Area
steps.text: Use the crop tool to ensure your face is centered and properly framed within the dimensions. Your face should occupy about 70-80% of the photo frame.

steps.name: Compress to Required Size
steps.text: Adjust the compression level to achieve a file size between 20KB and 50KB. PAN card applications typically accept photos in this range.

steps.name: Download and Verify
steps.text: Download your processed photo. Before submitting, verify that it meets PAN card's dimension and file size requirements.

faqs.question: What are the exact photo requirements for PAN card?
faqs.answer: For PAN card, the photograph should be in JPG format with dimensions 3.5cm (width) x 2.5cm (height) or 350x250 pixels. The file size should be between 20KB and 50KB, with a plain white background.

faqs.question: Can I wear glasses in my PAN card photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear glasses, but ensure there's no glare on the lenses and your eyes are clearly visible. Avoid tinted glasses or sunglasses.

faqs.question: What should be the background in my PAN card photo?
faqs.answer: PAN card requires a plain white background for the photograph. Avoid patterned, colored, or dark backgrounds as they may lead to rejection of your application.

faqs.question: Can I use a black and white photo for PAN card?
faqs.answer: No, PAN card applications require color photographs. Black and white photos are not accepted for PAN card applications.

faqs.question: How recent should my PAN card photo be?
faqs.answer: Your photo should be recent, typically not older than 6 months. It should accurately represent your current appearance as it will be used for identification purposes.

faqs.question: What if my PAN card photo is rejected?
faqs.answer: If your photo is rejected, check the error message for specific issues. Common problems include wrong dimensions, file size too large, poor image quality, or not following the background requirements. Use this tool to correct these issues.

pan-card-photo-resizer.seoDescription: PAN Card Photo Resizer - Free online tool to resize and compress photos for PAN card applications. Meet Income Tax Department's exact dimension and file size requirements with browser-based processing.

passport-photo-maker.whatItIs: This tool helps you create passport-style photos that meet Indian government standards for various official documents and forms. It provides the precise dimensions, background, and quality requirements needed for Indian passport applications and other official purposes.
features: Create passport photos to Indian standards (51mm x 51mm)
Resize and crop photos to exact passport dimensions
Compress photos to required file sizes

howToUse.name: How to Use Passport Photo Maker India

steps.name: Upload Your Photograph
steps.text: Select a recent photograph of yourself. The photo should be taken against a plain background, with your full face clearly visible from the front.

steps.name: Set Passport Dimensions
steps.text: The tool automatically configures the output to Indian passport standards (51mm x 51mm or 510x510 pixels). The aspect ratio will be maintained.

steps.name: Adjust Crop and Background
steps.text: Use the crop tool to ensure your face is centered and properly framed. If your photo doesn't have a white background, the tool can help adjust it to meet passport requirements.

steps.name: Compress to Required Size
steps.text: Adjust the compression level to achieve the required file size. For online submissions, this is typically between 20KB and 50KB.

steps.name: Download and Print
steps.text: Download your processed passport photo. For physical submissions, you can print it at the required size (51mm x 51mm) on matte photo paper.

faqs.question: What are the exact dimensions for Indian passport photo?
faqs.answer: Indian passport photos should be 51mm x 51mm (2 inches x 2 inches) in size. The digital version should be 510x510 pixels with a resolution of at least 300 DPI.

faqs.question: What should be the background color for Indian passport photo?
faqs.answer: Indian passport photos require a plain white background. The background should be uniform with no shadows, patterns, or other objects.

faqs.question: Can I wear glasses in my Indian passport photo?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can wear glasses, but ensure there's no glare on the lenses and your eyes are clearly visible. Avoid tinted glasses or sunglasses. Heavy-rimmed glasses should also be avoided.

faqs.question: What should I wear for my passport photo?
faqs.answer: Wear plain, light-colored clothing that contrasts with the white background. Avoid uniforms, dark colors, or patterns. Religious headwear is allowed if it doesn't cover your face.

faqs.question: How many passport photos do I need?
faqs.answer: For a new passport application, you typically need 2 identical photos. For renewal, you may need 1-2 photos. Always check the specific requirements for your application type.

faqs.question: Can I smile in my passport photo?
faqs.answer: You should maintain a neutral expression with your mouth closed. A slight smile is generally acceptable, but avoid broad smiles or any expression that might distort your facial features.

passport-photo-maker.seoDescription: Passport Photo Maker India - Free online tool to create passport-size photos (51mm x 51mm) for Indian passport applications. Meet exact dimension, background, and file size requirements with browser-based processing.

photo-compressor-under-50kb.whatItIs: This tool helps you compress your exam photos to under 50KB while maintaining the clarity needed for official form submissions. Many exam portals have strict file size limits, and this tool ensures your photos meet those requirements without losing important details.

howToUse.name: How to Use Photo Compressor Under 50KB

steps.name: Upload Your Photo
steps.text: Select the photo you need to compress. The photo should be a recent passport-size image with a plain background.

steps.name: Check Current File Size
steps.text: The tool will display the current file size of your photo. If it's already under 50KB, you may not need to compress it further.

steps.name: Adjust Compression Level
steps.text: Use the compression slider to reduce the file size to under 50KB. The tool shows the current file size in real-time as you adjust.

steps.name: Preview Quality
steps.text: Preview the compressed photo to ensure your face and details are still clear. If the photo appears blurry, reduce the compression slightly.

steps.name: Download Compressed Photo
steps.text: Once you're satisfied with the quality and file size, download the compressed photo. Verify that it's under 50KB before using it for your exam form.

faqs.question: Why do exam forms require photos under 50KB?
faqs.answer: Exam portals have file size limits to ensure smooth uploads and processing of thousands of applications. Photos under 50KB upload faster and don't consume excessive server storage.

faqs.question: Will compressing my photo affect its acceptance?
faqs.answer: As long as the compressed photo maintains clear facial details and meets the dimension requirements, it should be accepted. This tool uses smart compression to preserve important details.

faqs.question: What if my photo is still over 50KB after compression?
faqs.answer: If your photo is still too large, try cropping it more tightly around your face and shoulders, or start with a smaller original image. You can also try reducing the image dimensions slightly.

faqs.question: Can I compress a group photo for exam forms?
faqs.answer: No, exam forms require individual passport photos, not group photos. Use a recent individual passport-size photograph for compression.

faqs.question: What is the best original photo size for compression?
faqs.answer: Start with a high-resolution photo (at least 600x800 pixels) for best results. High-resolution photos compress better and maintain more detail at smaller file sizes.

faqs.question: Can I use this tool for non-exam photos?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use this tool to compress any photo to under 50KB for email attachments, web uploads, or any other purpose that requires smaller file sizes.

photo-compressor-under-50kb.seoDescription: Photo Compressor Under 50KB - Free online tool to compress exam photos for application forms. Reduce file size while maintaining clarity with browser-based processing.

form-image-size-checker.whatItIs: This tool helps you verify that your photos and signatures meet the exact dimension and file size requirements before you upload them to official exam forms. It checks all the critical specifications that exam portals validate, helping you avoid rejection due to incorrect image properties.
features: Check image dimensions in pixels and centimeters
Verify file size in KB and MB

howToUse.name: How to Use Form Image Size Checker

steps.name: Upload Your Image
steps.text: Drag and drop or select the photo or signature you want to check. The tool accepts JPG, PNG, and other common image formats.

steps.name: View Image Properties
steps.text: The tool will display all the properties of your image: dimensions (in pixels and centimeters), file size, format, and resolution (DPI).

steps.name: Select Exam Requirements
steps.text: Choose the specific exam you're applying for (e.g., UPSC, SSC, NEET) or enter the required dimensions and file size manually.

steps.name: Check Compliance
steps.text: The tool will compare your image properties against the selected exam's requirements and highlight any issues that need to be fixed.

steps.name: Fix Issues if Needed
steps.text: If your image doesn't meet the requirements, use the appropriate resizer or compressor tool to fix the issues, then re-check with this tool.

faqs.question: Why was my photo rejected by the exam portal?
faqs.answer: Common reasons for rejection include wrong dimensions, file size too large or too small, incorrect format, or poor image quality. Use this tool to check your photo against the exam's requirements before uploading.

faqs.question: What is DPI and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: DPI (dots per inch) measures the resolution of your image. Higher DPI means better quality. Most exam portals require at least 300 DPI for clear photos, but the file size also increases with higher DPI.

faqs.question: Can I check multiple images at once?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can upload and check multiple images in one session. This is useful for checking both your photo and signature, or for verifying images for multiple exam applications.

faqs.question: What if my image dimensions are in inches but the exam requires centimeters?
faqs.answer: This tool automatically converts between different units of measurement. It will display your image dimensions in both pixels and centimeters, making it easy to compare against any exam's requirements.

faqs.question: How accurate is the size checking?
faqs.answer: The tool provides precise measurements of your image properties. The accuracy depends on the metadata stored in your image file, which is typically very reliable for digital photos.

faqs.question: Can I use this tool for non-exam images?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use this tool to check the properties of any image, whether for exams, job applications, or any other purpose that has specific image requirements.

form-image-size-checker.seoDescription: Form Image Size Checker - Free online tool to verify photo and signature dimensions, file size, format, and DPI for exam forms. Ensure compliance with exact requirements before uploading.

photo-name-date-editor.whatItIs: This tool helps you add your name and date to your exam photos when the application form specifically requires it. Some exam portals ask for photos with the candidate's name and date printed on them, and this tool makes it easy to add this text while maintaining the photo's compliance with other requirements.
features: Add text (name, date) to exam photos
Customize font, size, and color of text
Position text anywhere on the photo

howToUse.name: How to Use Photo Name Date Editor

steps.name: Upload Your Photo
steps.text: Select the photo you need to edit. The photo should already meet the dimension and file size requirements for your exam.

steps.name: Enter Text Details
steps.text: Type your name and the required date in the text fields. You can also add any other text that your specific exam form requires.

steps.name: Customize Text Appearance
steps.text: Choose the font, size, color, and position for your text. For most exam photos, use a clear, readable font in black color. Position the text where it won't obscure your face.

steps.name: Adjust Text Position
steps.text: Drag the text to the desired position on your photo. Common positions are at the bottom or side of the photo, but always check your exam's specific requirements.

steps.name: Download Edited Photo
steps.text: Download your edited photo. Verify that the text is clear and legible, and that the photo still meets all other requirements (dimensions, file size, etc.).

faqs.question: Which exams require name and date on photos?
faqs.answer: Some state-level exams and certain university entrance tests require the candidate's name and date to be printed on the photo. Always check your specific exam notification for this requirement.

faqs.question: Where should I place the text on my photo?
faqs.answer: The text should be placed where it doesn't obscure your face or any important features. Common positions are at the bottom of the photo or along one side. Avoid placing text over your face, eyes, or other identifying features.

faqs.question: What font and size should I use?
faqs.answer: Use a clear, sans-serif font like Arial or Helvetica. The font size should be large enough to be readable but not so large that it dominates the photo. Typically, 12-18pt font works well.

faqs.question: Can I add other information besides name and date?
faqs.answer: You can add any text that your specific exam form requires. However, only add what's explicitly asked for, as extra text might cause the photo to be rejected.

faqs.question: Will adding text affect my photo's file size?
faqs.answer: Adding text to your photo may slightly increase the file size. After adding text, check the file size and compress if necessary to meet your exam's requirements.

faqs.question: What if the text obscures part of my photo?
faqs.answer: If the text obscures important parts of your photo, reposition it to a less intrusive location. The text should be clearly visible but not cover your face, eyes, or other identifying features.

photo-name-date-editor.seoDescription: Photo Name Date Editor - Free online tool to add name and date text to exam photos. Customize font, size, and position while maintaining compliance with exam requirements.

jpg-to-pdf-exam-forms.whatItIs: This tool helps you convert your exam photos and documents from JPG format to PDF, which is often required for online form submissions. It creates PDF files that are optimized for exam portals, with the right file size and quality to ensure smooth uploads.
features: Convert JPG images to PDF format
Compress PDF to exam-friendly file sizes
Supports multiple images in one PDF

howToUse.name: How to Use JPG to PDF for Exam Forms

steps.name: Upload Your JPG Image
steps.text: Select the JPG image you want to convert to PDF. This could be your exam photo, signature, or any other document required for your application.

steps.name: Set PDF Options
steps.text: Choose your PDF settings. For exam forms, select the appropriate page size (usually A4 or the size of your image) and orientation (portrait or landscape).

steps.name: Adjust Quality and File Size
steps.text: Adjust the quality settings to achieve the desired file size. Exam portals often have PDF size limits, typically under 200KB or 500KB.

steps.name: Add Multiple Images (if needed)
steps.text: If you need to combine multiple images (e.g., photo and signature) into one PDF, upload all the images. The tool will create a multi-page PDF or arrange them on a single page.

steps.name: Download PDF
steps.text: Download your PDF file. Verify that it opens correctly and meets the file size requirements for your exam form before uploading.

faqs.question: Why do exam forms require PDF instead of JPG?
faqs.answer: PDF format is often preferred for official documents because it preserves the layout, prevents easy editing, and can combine multiple pages or images into one file. Some exam portals specifically require PDF for consistency.

faqs.question: What PDF size is acceptable for exam forms?
faqs.answer: Most exam portals accept PDF files under 200KB or 500KB. Some may have larger limits. Always check your specific exam's requirements. This tool helps you create PDFs within these limits.

faqs.question: Can I convert multiple JPGs to one PDF?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can upload multiple JPG images and the tool will combine them into a single PDF file. This is useful for exams that require both a photo and signature in one document.

faqs.question: Will the PDF quality be good enough for exam forms?
faqs.answer: Yes, the tool maintains high quality in the PDF conversion. The quality settings are optimized for exam forms, ensuring that your photos and documents remain clear and legible.

faqs.question: Can I edit the PDF after conversion?
faqs.answer: The PDF created by this tool is a standard PDF that can be opened and viewed by any PDF reader. However, for security, some exam portals may require the PDF to be non-editable. Always check your exam's specific requirements.

faqs.question: What if my PDF is still too large?
faqs.answer: If your PDF is still too large, try reducing the quality settings further, or compress the original JPG images before converting them to PDF. You can also try creating separate PDFs for each image.

jpg-to-pdf-exam-forms.seoDescription: JPG to PDF for Exam Forms - Free online tool to convert exam photos and documents to PDF format. Create optimized PDFs that meet exam portal requirements with browser-based processing.

pdf-compressor-under-200kb.whatItIs: This tool helps you compress your PDF documents to under 200KB, which is a common file size limit for many exam forms, job applications, and government portals. It reduces the file size while maintaining the document's readability and quality.

howToUse.name: How to Use PDF Compressor Under 200KB

steps.name: Upload Your PDF
steps.text: Select the PDF file you need to compress. This could be your exam form, certificate, or any other document required for your application.

steps.name: Check Current File Size
steps.text: The tool will display the current file size of your PDF. If it's already under 200KB, you may not need to compress it further.

steps.name: Adjust Compression Level
steps.text: Use the compression slider to reduce the file size to under 200KB. The tool shows the current file size in real-time as you adjust.

steps.name: Preview Compressed PDF
steps.text: Preview the compressed PDF to ensure all text and images are still readable. If the quality is too low, reduce the compression slightly.

steps.name: Download Compressed PDF
steps.text: Once you're satisfied with the quality and file size, download the compressed PDF. Verify that it's under 200KB and all content is clear before using it for your exam form.

faqs.question: Why do exam forms require PDFs under 200KB?
faqs.answer: Exam portals have file size limits to ensure smooth uploads and processing of thousands of applications. PDFs under 200KB upload faster and don't consume excessive server storage, especially when many candidates are submitting simultaneously.

faqs.question: Will compressing my PDF affect its acceptance?
faqs.answer: As long as the compressed PDF maintains readable text and clear images, it should be accepted. This tool uses smart compression to preserve important content while reducing file size.

faqs.question: What if my PDF is still over 200KB after compression?
faqs.answer: If your PDF is still too large, try compressing the original images within the PDF before creating it, or split the PDF into smaller files. You can also try reducing the resolution of images in the PDF.

faqs.question: Can I compress a scanned PDF?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can compress scanned PDFs. Scanned documents often have large file sizes due to high-resolution images. This tool can significantly reduce the file size while maintaining readability.

faqs.question: What is the best original PDF size for compression?
faqs.answer: Start with a PDF that has reasonable quality. If you're creating the PDF from images, use images that are appropriately sized (not excessively large) for best compression results.

faqs.question: Can I use this tool for non-exam PDFs?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use this tool to compress any PDF to under 200KB for email attachments, web uploads, or any other purpose that has file size limits.

faqs.question: What are the exact photo requirements for SSC MTS 2026?
faqs.answer: For SSC MTS 2026, the photograph must be in JPEG format, with dimensions of 3.5 cm (width) x 4.5 cm (height), and a file size between 20 KB and 50 KB. The background should be plain white.

faqs.question: What are the signature requirements for SSC MTS?
faqs.answer: The signature should be in JPEG format with a file size between 10 KB and 20 KB. It should be clear and legible, in your normal handwriting on white paper with black ink.

faqs.question: Can I use a colored background for SSC MTS photo?
faqs.answer: No, SSC MTS requires a plain white background for the passport photograph. Any colored or patterned background may lead to rejection of your application.

faqs.question: What should I wear for my SSC MTS photo?
faqs.answer: Wear plain, light-colored clothing that contrasts with the white background. Avoid uniforms, dark colors, or patterns. Religious headwear is allowed if it doesn't cover your face.

faqs.question: How can I ensure my SSC MTS photo is accepted?
faqs.answer: Follow these guidelines: recent photo (not older than 6 months), plain white background, neutral expression, no shadows, proper lighting, and clear facial features. Use this tool to ensure correct dimensions and file size.

faqs.question: What if my SSC MTS photo is rejected?
faqs.answer: If your photo is rejected, check the error message for specific issues. Common problems include wrong dimensions, file size too large, poor image quality, or not following the background requirements. Use this tool to correct these issues.

faqs.question: What is the photo size limit for RRB Group D?
faqs.answer: For RRB Group D, the passport photograph should be between 20 KB and 50 KB in JPEG format. The exact dimensions may vary, but typically around 3.5cm x 4.5cm.

faqs.question: What is the signature size limit for RRB Group D?
faqs.answer: The signature file for RRB Group D should be between 10 KB and 20 KB in JPEG format. The signature should be clear and legible, in your normal handwriting.

faqs.question: Can I wear a cap in my RRB Group D photo?
faqs.answer: No, headwear including caps, hats, or religious headwear (unless it doesn't cover the face) is not permitted in RRB Group D application photos. Your full face and hairline should be clearly visible.

faqs.question: What should be the background in my RRB Group D photo?
faqs.answer: RRB Group D requires a plain white background for the passport photograph. Avoid patterned, colored, or dark backgrounds as they may lead to rejection of your application.
